summary: Adjust Auto Scaling capacity and behavior once an ASG exists: min/max/desired capacity, bounded target-tracking scaling policies, guarded instance refreshes, suspending and resuming ASG processes during maintenance, scheduled scaling for recurring batch windows, and reversibly removing a policy. The first stand-up of an ASG stays with an engineer because AMI, subnet, IAM, security-group, and sizing choices are architecture decisions. Every operation changes capacity, cost, or behavior, so all stay human-approved within policy caps; none auto-apply.

operations.title: Adjust min / max / desired capacity on an ASG
operations.summary: Change how many servers this group runs (minimum, maximum, and target). Takes effect right away with no downtime.
operations.description: In-place update of an existing ASG's capacity bounds (min_size, max_size, and/or desired_capacity) — the single most common day-2 ask, e.g. bump the app tier from 2 to 4 under load. No launch-template change and no replacement; scale-out launches real instances (cost delta) and scale-in can terminate a serving instance, so it is capped by policy and stays human-approved.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling Group
params.help: The ASG whose capacity you want to change.

params.name: new_desired_capacity
params.label: Desired capacity
params.help: New running instance count. Must sit within the effective min/max after this change.

params.name: new_min_size
params.label: Minimum size
params.help: Leave blank to keep the current minimum. Must be <= max size.

params.name: new_max_size
params.label: Maximum size
params.help: Leave blank to keep the current maximum. Above the policy cap of 20 the request routes to an engineer.

operations.title: Add a target-tracking scaling policy to an ASG
operations.summary: Add a policy that automatically scales this group to hold a target, like average CPU. Existing size limits still cap how far it scales.
operations.description: Attach a new aws_autoscaling_policy (policy_type=TargetTrackingScaling) to an existing ASG — the AWS-recommended scaling mode, e.g. hold average CPU at 60%. Additive (a new block; does not modify the ASG's own attributes) and bounded: metric picked from an allowlist and the ASG's existing max_size still caps how far it can scale. Reversible by deleting the policy.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling Group
params.help: The ASG the new scaling policy attaches to.

params.name: predefined_metric_type
params.label: Metric to track
params.help: The predefined metric the policy holds at the target value.

params.name: target_value
params.label: Target value
params.help: The value the metric is held at. For CPU utilisation use 10-90 (percent); for request-count use requests-per-target.

params.name: policy_name
params.label: Policy name
params.help: Name for the new policy. Defaults to tt-<metric>.

params.name: disable_scale_in
params.label: Scale-out only
params.help: If true the policy only scales out and never scales in.

operations.title: Start a rolling instance refresh on an ASG
operations.summary: Roll every server in this group onto its current setup, a few at a time. Can't be undone once it starts; brief interruption.
operations.description: Configure/trigger an instance_refresh on an existing ASG to roll every instance onto the ASG's current launch-template configuration, honouring a minimum-healthy-percentage so the tier keeps serving throughout. Used to bake in a config already published by an engineer. It replaces instances in a rolling fashion (brief per-instance interruption) and is not cleanly undoable once instances are cycled, so it stays capped and human-approved.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling Group
params.help: The ASG whose instances will be rolled.

params.name: strategy
params.label: Refresh strategy
params.help: Instance-refresh strategy. Only Rolling is supported.

params.name: min_healthy_percentage
params.label: Minimum healthy percentage
params.help: How much of the ASG must stay healthy during the roll. Lower = faster but less capacity headroom.

params.name: instance_warmup
params.label: Instance warmup (seconds)
params.help: Seconds a replaced instance warms up before the refresh counts it healthy and proceeds.

operations.title: Suspend / resume ASG scaling processes
operations.summary: Pause or resume this group's automatic scaling and health actions, for example during a maintenance window.
operations.description: Set the ASG's suspended_processes list to pause selected Auto Scaling activities (e.g. suspend Launch, Terminate, HealthCheck, ReplaceUnhealthy during a maintenance window so the ASG does not fight a manual intervention), then clear the list to resume. In-place, fully reversible, no replacement — but suspending HealthCheck/ReplaceUnhealthy leaves unhealthy instances in service, so it stays guarded. Set to an empty list to resume all processes.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling Group
params.help: The ASG whose processes you want to suspend or resume.

params.name: new_suspended_processes
params.label: Processes to suspend
params.help: Processes to suspend. Send an empty list to resume everything.

operations.title: Add a scheduled scaling action to an ASG
operations.summary: Schedule this group to change capacity at set times, e.g. scale up before a nightly batch and back down after.
operations.description: Add a new aws_autoscaling_schedule so the ASG changes capacity on a timer — the classic batch pattern (scale a tier up before a recurring run, back down after). Additive and reversible (delete the schedule); the desired/max it can set share the same policy caps as capacity changes, so a schedule cannot be used to bypass the ceiling. Cron is validated in policy.yaml.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling Group
params.help: The ASG the scheduled action attaches to.

params.name: scheduled_action_name
params.label: Action name
params.help: Unique name for the scheduled action within this ASG.

params.name: desired_capacity
params.label: Desired capacity at fire time
params.help: Instance count the action sets when it fires. Capped by the same policy ceiling as manual capacity changes.

params.name: recurrence
params.label: Recurrence (cron)
params.help: 5-field cron for when the action fires, e.g. '0 22 * * *' for 22:00 daily.

params.name: time_zone
params.label: Time zone
params.help: IANA timezone the cron is evaluated in.

operations.title: Remove a scaling policy from an ASG
operations.summary: Remove one scaling policy so this group no longer fires it. Can't be undone; re-adding it later is a fresh policy.
operations.description: Destroy one specifically-identified aws_autoscaling_policy chosen from inventory (e.g. retire a superseded target-tracking or step policy). The L1 picks the exact policy — never free-typed — and the codemod removes that one block. It destroys only a controller (no compute or state) and reduces autonomous behaviour, but removing the wrong policy can leave the tier unable to scale under load, so it is guarded and requires a typed confirmation.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: The exact scaling policy to remove, selected from the ASG's current policies.

params.name: confirm_remove_policy
params.label: Confirm removal
params.help: Typed confirmation that this policy will be destroyed and the ASG will no longer scale on it.

operations.title: Enable or disable a scaling policy
operations.summary: Pause or resume a scaling policy without deleting it. Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Flip the `enabled` flag on an aws_autoscaling_policy to pause or resume it without deleting it. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the scaling policy to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Enabled
params.help: true resumes the policy; false pauses it. The policy is retained either way.

operations.title: Set simple-scaling cooldown
operations.summary: Set how long the group waits between scaling actions (simple scaling). Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Set `cooldown` (seconds between scaling activities, SimpleScaling only) on an aws_autoscaling_policy. Bounded threshold, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the SimpleScaling policy to update.

params.name: cooldown
params.label: Cooldown (seconds)
params.help: Seconds to wait after a scaling activity before another can start. 0–3600.

operations.title: Set estimated instance warmup
operations.summary: Set how long a new server warms up before its metrics count toward scaling. Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Set `estimated_instance_warmup` (seconds until a new instance's metrics count toward the ASG) on an aws_autoscaling_policy. Bounded threshold, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the scaling policy to update.

params.name: estimated_instance_warmup
params.label: Warmup (seconds)
params.help: Seconds before a newly launched instance's metrics are included in aggregate. 0–3600.

operations.title: Set minimum adjustment magnitude
operations.summary: Set the fewest servers a percentage-based scaling step may add or remove at once. Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Set `min_adjustment_magnitude` (minimum instances to scale when adjustment_type is PercentChangeInCapacity) on an aws_autoscaling_policy. Bounded threshold, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the scaling policy to update.

params.name: min_adjustment_magnitude
params.label: Minimum adjustment magnitude
params.help: Floor on the number of instances a PercentChangeInCapacity step will move. 0 disables the floor.

operations.title: Set step-scaling metric aggregation type
operations.summary: Choose how the metric is summarized (minimum, maximum, or average) for step-scaling decisions. Reversible.
operations.description: Set `metric_aggregation_type` (how CloudWatch aggregates the metric for StepScaling decisions) on an aws_autoscaling_policy. Bounded enum, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the StepScaling policy to update.

params.name: metric_aggregation_type
params.label: Aggregation type
params.help: How the trigger metric is aggregated over the evaluation window.

operations.title: Set scaling adjustment type
operations.summary: Choose how a scaling step is applied: add a fixed number, set an exact number, or a percentage. Changes future scaling.
operations.description: Set `adjustment_type`, which reinterprets how scaling_adjustment is applied (add N, set exactly N, or percent). In-place and reversible with no downtime, but it changes scaling behavior on the next trigger — guardrailed.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the scaling policy to update.

params.name: adjustment_type
params.label: Adjustment type
params.help: Determines whether scaling_adjustment adds instances, sets an exact count, or a percentage. Verify scaling_adjustment still makes sense afterward.

operations.title: Set simple-scaling adjustment amount
operations.summary: Set how much this policy scales the group each time it fires. Directly changes capacity on the next trigger.
operations.description: Set `scaling_adjustment` (SimpleScaling amount to scale, interpreted per adjustment_type) on an aws_autoscaling_policy. In-place and reversible with no downtime, but it directly controls capacity change on the next trigger — guardrailed.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the SimpleScaling policy to update.

params.name: scaling_adjustment
params.label: Scaling adjustment
params.help: Instances (or percent, per adjustment_type) to add (positive) or remove (negative) per trigger. -100 to 100.

operations.title: Request another change to Auto Scaling policy
operations.summary: Request a scaling-policy change that isn't offered as a form; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any aws_autoscaling_policy change not offered as a bounded form (e.g. policy_type switch, step_adjustment / target_tracking / predictive_scaling blocks, rename). An engineer authors the Terraform.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: The scaling policy this request is about, when the estate already tracks one. Leave blank when describing one that is not tracked yet.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Set health check type on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Choose whether health is judged by the server or the load balancer. The load-balancer option can replace servers it marks unhealthy.
operations.description: Switch health_check_type between EC2 and ELB. ELB uses load-balancer health checks, which can mark instances unhealthy and trigger replacement, so this is guardrailed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group whose health-check type will change, picked from groups already managed in Terraform so the change cannot target an unknown or hand-built group.

params.name: health_check_type
params.label: Health check type
params.help: ELB marks instances unhealthy on load-balancer health-check failure, which can trigger replacement.

operations.title: Set default cooldown on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Set the default wait between scaling actions for this group. Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Adjust default_cooldown, the seconds between scaling activities. Reversible timing threshold, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group whose scaling cooldown you are adjusting; the dropdown lists only Terraform-managed groups, so a free-typed name can never point at the wrong resource.

params.name: default_cooldown
params.label: Default cooldown (seconds)
params.help: Seconds between scaling activities. Provider default is 300.

operations.title: Set health check grace period on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Set how long after a server launches before health checks begin. Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Adjust health_check_grace_period, the seconds after launch before health checks begin. Reversible timing threshold, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group whose health-check grace period you are adjusting, selected from Terraform inventory so only groups the estate already manages can be modified.

params.name: health_check_grace_period
params.label: Health check grace period (seconds)
params.help: Grace period before health checks start after an instance launches. Provider default is 300.

operations.title: Toggle capacity rebalance on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Turn on or off proactively replacing Spot servers that are at risk of interruption. Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Enable or disable capacity_rebalance so the group proactively replaces Spot instances at elevated interruption risk. Reversible bool toggle, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group to toggle capacity rebalancing on, chosen from Terraform-managed groups so the change is scoped to a known resource.

params.name: capacity_rebalance
params.label: Capacity rebalance
params.help: When true, the group acts on EC2 Spot rebalance recommendations and launches a replacement before an at-risk Spot instance is interrupted; the allowlist permits only true or false, so nothing else about the group can change.

operations.title: Toggle default scale-in protection on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Turn on or off protecting new servers in this group from being removed when it scales in. Reversible.
operations.description: Enable or disable protect_from_scale_in, the default instance scale-in protection for new instances. Reversible bool toggle, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group whose default scale-in protection you are toggling, picked from Terraform inventory so only managed groups are selectable.

params.name: protect_from_scale_in
params.label: Default scale-in protection
params.help: When true, instances the group launches from now on are protected from termination during scale-in events (manual termination and unhealthy-instance replacement still apply); only true or false is accepted.

operations.title: Set default instance warmup on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Set how long a new server warms up before it counts toward this group's metrics. Reversible; no downtime.
operations.description: Adjust default_instance_warmup, the seconds before a new instance contributes to aggregated metrics. Reversible timing threshold, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group whose default instance warmup you are setting, limited to groups already in Terraform inventory so the timing change lands on a known resource.

params.name: default_instance_warmup
params.label: Default instance warmup (seconds)
params.help: Warm-up time before an instance is counted toward aggregated instance metrics.

operations.title: Set max instance lifetime on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Set a maximum age after which servers are automatically replaced. Drives rolling replacement, cycling running servers over time.
operations.description: Set max_instance_lifetime so instances older than the limit are rolling-replaced. Stateful behavior change that drives instance churn, so guardrailed. Range 86400-31536000 seconds; disabling (0) is engineer-only.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group whose maximum instance lifetime you are setting; selection is restricted to Terraform-managed groups so the rolling-replacement churn this drives applies only to a known resource.

params.name: max_instance_lifetime
params.label: Max instance lifetime (seconds)
params.help: Instances older than this are replaced via rolling activity. Valid range 86400-31536000. Use the engineer request to set 0 (disable).

operations.title: Set termination policies on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Choose the order that decides which servers are removed first when scaling in. It changes which servers are terminated, with no downtime.
operations.description: Set the ordered termination_policies that decide which instances are selected on scale-in. Reversible config change, no downtime, but affects which instances are terminated, so guardrailed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group whose scale-in termination order you are changing, chosen from Terraform inventory so this guardrailed change targets only a managed group.

params.name: termination_policies
params.label: Termination policies (ordered)
params.help: Priority order used to pick instances for scale-in.

operations.title: Enable group metrics on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Turn on detailed one-minute group metrics for monitoring. Reversible; no effect on running servers.
operations.description: Set enabled_metrics to collect CloudWatch group metrics at 1-minute granularity. Observability-only, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group to enable CloudWatch group metrics on, selected from Terraform-managed groups; the change is observability-only for that group.

params.name: enabled_metrics
params.label: Group metrics to enable
params.help: CloudWatch group metrics collected at 1-minute granularity.

operations.title: Add a tag to Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Add a metadata tag to this group. No effect on how it runs; reversible.
operations.description: Append a tag block (key/value/propagate_at_launch) to the group. Cosmetic metadata change, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group receiving the new tag, picked from Terraform inventory so the metadata lands on a known group.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: The tag's key (name), restricted to 1-128 characters from a tag-safe character set (letters, digits, and _ + . : / @ -; no spaces or =), so it cannot break the rendered configuration.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: The tag's value, limited to at most 256 characters from the set AWS allows for tag values (it may be empty), matching what EC2 accepts for tags.

params.name: propagate_at_launch
params.label: Propagate at launch
params.help: Whether the tag is applied to instances launched by the group.

operations.title: Remove a tag from Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Remove a metadata tag from this group. No effect on how it runs; reversible.
operations.description: Remove a tag block by key from the group. Cosmetic metadata change, reversible by re-adding, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group to remove the tag from, chosen from Terraform inventory so only a managed group's metadata changes.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: The key of the existing tag block to delete, constrained to AWS's tag-key character set and 1-128 length; removal only touches metadata and is reversible by re-adding the tag.

operations.title: Request another change to Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Request a group change that isn't offered as a form; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change to an Auto Scaling group not offered as a bounded self-service form. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The Auto Scaling group this request is about, when the estate already tracks one. Leave blank when describing one that is not tracked yet.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Set Availability Zone capacity distribution strategy
operations.summary: Choose how new servers are spread across Availability Zones. Reversible; affects the resilience of future launches.
operations.description: Set availability_zone_distribution.capacity_distribution_strategy on an ASG - balanced-only or balanced-best-effort (provider default) - controlling how new capacity is spread across Availability Zones. In-place and reversible with no downtime; because it changes cross-AZ capacity balancing it stays guardrailed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose AZ distribution strategy you want to set.

params.name: capacity_distribution_strategy
params.label: Capacity distribution strategy
params.help: How capacity is distributed across Availability Zones. Provider default is balanced-best-effort.

operations.title: Set capacity reservation preference on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Choose how this group uses On-Demand Capacity Reservations for new servers. Reversible; affects cost and placement.
operations.description: Set capacity_reservation_specification.capacity_reservation_preference on an ASG (default, capacity-reservations-only, capacity-reservations-first, or none), controlling how the group prioritizes On-Demand Capacity Reservations for future launches. In-place and reversible; the optional capacity_reservation_target (specific reservation IDs/ARNs) is not exposed here and stays reachable via the engineer catch-all. Guardrailed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose capacity reservation preference you want to set.

params.name: capacity_reservation_preference
params.label: Capacity reservation preference
params.help: How the ASG prioritizes On-Demand Capacity Reservations for future launches. Provider default is default.

operations.title: Set desired capacity type on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Choose whether target capacity is counted in servers, vCPUs, or memory. A mismatch can over- or under-provision.
operations.description: Set desired_capacity_type - the unit desired_capacity is measured in: units, vcpu, or memory-mib. Supported for attribute-based instance type selection. In-place and reversible, but a unit/value mismatch can under- or over-provision, so guardrailed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose desired capacity type you want to set.

params.name: desired_capacity_type
params.label: Desired capacity type
params.help: Unit desired_capacity is measured in. Applies to attribute-based instance type selection.

operations.title: Toggle ignore failed scaling activities
operations.summary: Turn on or off ignoring failed scaling attempts during apply. Turning it on can hide real scaling failures.
operations.description: Enable or disable ignore_failed_scaling_activities (provider default false). When true, Terraform ignores failed scaling activities while waiting for capacity - reversible bool toggle with no downtime, but it masks failed scaling activities, so guardrailed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose ignore_failed_scaling_activities flag you want to set.

params.name: ignore_failed_scaling_activities
params.label: Ignore failed scaling activities
params.help: When true, failed scaling activities are ignored while waiting for capacity. Provider default is false.

operations.title: Set metrics granularity on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Set how often group metrics are collected (one minute). Reversible; no effect on running servers.
operations.description: Set metrics_granularity, the granularity of ASG CloudWatch metrics collection. The only valid (and default) value is 1Minute. In-place, reversible, no downtime - a fully bounded, cosmetic setting.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose metrics granularity you want to set.

params.name: metrics_granularity
params.label: Metrics granularity
params.help: Granularity of collected ASG metrics. The only valid (and default) value is 1Minute.

operations.title: Set wait-for-capacity timeout on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Set how long an apply waits for new servers to become healthy before giving up. Affects the apply only, not live servers.
operations.description: Set wait_for_capacity_timeout, the maximum Golang-duration time (e.g. 10m) Terraform waits for ASG instances to become healthy during apply; 0 disables capacity waiting. Governs only apply-time waiting, not live AWS state - in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose wait-for-capacity timeout you want to set.

params.name: wait_for_capacity_timeout
params.label: Wait-for-capacity timeout
params.help: Golang duration string (e.g. 10m) for how long apply waits for healthy capacity; 0 disables waiting. Apply-time only - no effect on live AWS state.

operations.title: Set launch template version on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Choose which launch-template version new servers use. Existing servers are unchanged and only future launches use the new version.
operations.description: Set the launch_template.version the ASG uses for future launches - a specific version number, $Latest, or $Default (provider default). Only the version is repointed; the launch template id/name is unchanged and stays engineer-only via the catch-all. Affects future launches only - in-place, reversible, no downtime - but changing which template version launches new instances stays guardrailed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG (using a launch_template) whose template version you want to set.

params.name: launch_template_version
params.label: Launch template version
params.help: Launch template version future instances use: a version number, $Latest, or $Default. Only the version is changed; the template id/name is untouched.

operations.title: Toggle force delete on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Turn on or off letting this group be deleted without gracefully shutting servers down first. Removes a safety guardrail; engineer-only.
operations.description: Enable or disable force_delete, which allows a later deletion of the ASG to proceed without waiting for all instances to terminate gracefully. Toggling the flag is in-place and reversible, but enabling it removes a data-safety guardrail (instances can be force-terminated on destroy), so every application is engineer-reviewed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose force_delete flag you want to set.

params.name: force_delete
params.label: Force delete
params.help: When true, a later ASG delete proceeds without waiting for instances to terminate gracefully. Removes a data-safety guardrail.

operations.title: Toggle force delete warm pool on Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Turn on or off letting deletion skip a graceful shutdown of warm-pool servers. Removes a safety guardrail; engineer-only.
operations.description: Enable or disable force_delete_warm_pool, which allows a later deletion of the ASG to proceed without waiting for all warm-pool instances to terminate gracefully. Same guardrail-removal pattern as force_delete, scoped to warm-pool instances, so every application is engineer-reviewed.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The ASG whose force_delete_warm_pool flag you want to set.

params.name: force_delete_warm_pool
params.label: Force delete warm pool
params.help: When true, a later ASG delete proceeds without waiting for warm-pool instances to terminate gracefully. Removes a data-safety guardrail.

operations.title: Toggle scale-in on a target-tracking scaling policy
operations.summary: Stop or allow a target-tracking policy from scaling in. Leaving scale-in off can keep paying for extra servers.
operations.description: Set target_tracking_configuration.disable_scale_in on an existing TargetTrackingScaling aws_autoscaling_policy. When true the policy only scales out and never scales in (risking sustained overprovisioning/cost if left on); false lets it scale in again. In-place, reversible, no downtime; guardrailed because it changes live scaling behaviour.

params.name: policy
params.label: Scaling policy
params.help: Pick the TargetTrackingScaling policy to update.

params.name: disable_scale_in
params.label: Disable scale-in
params.help: true = scale-out only (never scales in); false = allow scale-in. Provider default is false.

operations.title: Provision a new Auto Scaling group
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Auto Scaling group; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new group as a complete specification: capacity bounds, network placement, health checking, and the launch template it runs. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: group_name
params.label: Group name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like app-tier-asg. Names the group in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: min_size
params.label: Minimum size
params.help: Fewest instances the group ever runs.

params.name: max_size
params.label: Maximum size
params.help: Most instances the group may scale up to.

params.name: desired_capacity
params.label: Desired capacity
params.help: Starting instance count; leave blank to let AWS use the minimum size.

params.name: subnets
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Where new instances launch. Pick subnets spanning at least two Availability Zones for resilience.

params.name: health_check_type
params.label: Health check type
params.help: ELB uses load-balancer health checks and can trigger replacement; pick it once a target group is attached.

params.name: health_check_grace_period
params.label: Health check grace period (seconds)
params.help: Seconds after launch before health checks begin.

params.name: launch_template_id
params.label: Launch template
params.help: The engineer picks and validates the launch template during review — it fixes the AMI, instance type, and IAM profile new instances launch with.

params.name: launch_template_version
params.label: Launch template version
params.help: Which version of the launch template new instances use.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag value
params.help: Shown as the Name tag on the group and, when propagated, on every instance it launches.

params.name: tag_key_name
params.label: Name tag key
params.help: Always Name — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: tag_propagate
params.label: Propagate tag to instances
params.help: Always propagated to launched instances — written into the draft, never asked.

decisions: Launch template choice and validation (AMI, instance type, IAM profile)
Attaching to a load balancer target group is a follow-up step
Description and person-in-charge tags: add via the existing tag operation after creation — an Auto Scaling group's tags are a repeated block, not a map, so only the Name tag is seeded here
Mixed instances policy, warm pool, and instance refresh settings are engineer decisions

operations.title: Provision a new scaling policy
operations.summary: Request a new target-tracking scaling policy on an existing group; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_autoscaling_policy as a complete specification: the target-tracking metric and value it holds. Target tracking is the AWS-recommended scaling mode. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: policy_name
params.label: Policy name
params.help: Name for the new policy, like tt-cpu. Defaults to tt-<metric>.

params.name: asg
params.label: Auto Scaling group
params.help: The group this policy scales. Create the group first if the list is empty.

params.name: policy_type
params.label: Policy type
params.help: Always target tracking, the AWS-recommended mode — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: predefined_metric_type
params.label: Metric to track
params.help: The predefined metric the policy holds at the target value.

params.name: resource_label
params.label: Resource label
params.help: Required only for ALBRequestCountPerTarget: the target group's resource label, e.g. app/my-alb/50dc6c495c0c9188/targetgroup/my-targets/73e2d6bc24d8a067.

params.name: target_value
params.label: Target value
params.help: The value the metric is held at. For CPU utilisation use 10-90 (percent); for request-count use requests-per-target.

params.name: disable_scale_in
params.label: Scale-out only
params.help: If true the policy only scales out and never scales in.

decisions: Step scaling and predictive scaling policy types are an engineer decision — this form covers the recommended target-tracking case only
A customized (non-predefined) metric specification is an engineer decision
